title: FuelCell Energy Q1 2024 Earnings Call Summary
text: FuelCell Energy Q1 2024 (ended Jan 31, 2024) revenues were $16.7 million, down from $37.1 million prior year. Net loss was $44.4 million vs $21.1 million in Q1 2023. Loss per share remained at $(0.05), unchanged YoY despite higher absolute losses. Adjusted EBITDA declined to $(29.1) million from $(14.4) million. Cash position was strong at $348.8 million as of January 31, 2024. CEO Jason Few emphasized progress on powerhouse business strategy with wins: placing two Derby, Connecticut projects into service and advancing solid oxide manufacturing in Calgary. Generation portfolio expanded to 62.8 megawatts from 43.7 megawatts. Revenue breakdown: Generation increased 10% to $10.5 million, Service revenues declined significantly to $1.6 million from $13.9 million (completion of module replacement cycles), Advanced Technology contract revenues flat at $4.6 million. Backlog decreased slightly to $1.03 billion from $1.06 billion due to revenue recognition activities. New agreements: Ukraine Project partnering with U.S. Department of State on solid oxide electrolysis with nuclear reactors. UConn Agreement: eight-year PPA for four 250-kW solid oxide fuel cell units.
